agent_output: {"executive_summary":"This synchronous-chain architecture (Order→Payment→Stripe) creates a fragile call path where any single failure cascades upstream, while the fire-and-forget notification path silently loses emails. The design needs circuit breakers, idempotency keys, transactional outbox for notifications, and a saga/compensation pattern for the Order-Payment interaction to achieve production-grade resilience.","architecture":{"components":[{"name":"Order Service","responsibility":"Accepts customer orders, orchestrates payment charging, and triggers post-order workflows (notifications). Owns order state machine (CREATED → PAYMENT_PENDING → CONFIRMED → FAILED).","boundaries":"Owns order aggregate. Must not leak payment internals into order model. Responsible for retry/compensation decisions."},{"name":"Payment Service","responsibility":"Abstracts payment gateway interactions. Owns payment state (INITIATED → CHARGED → FAILED → REFUNDED). Provides idempotent charge endpoint.","boundaries":"Owns payment aggregate. Encapsulates Stripe-specific logic behind anti-corruption layer. Exposes gateway-agnostic payment API."},{"name":"Stripe (Third-Party Gateway)","responsibility":"Processes actual card charges. External, uncontrollable dependency.","boundaries":"Completely outside our operational control. Latency, availability, and behavior are unpredictable. Must be treated as inherently unreliable."},{"name":"Notification Service","responsibility":"Sends confirmation emails and other customer communications.","boundaries":"Owns notification templates and delivery tracking. Should be fully decoupled from order success/failure path."}],"interfaces":[{"from":"Order Service","to":"Payment Service","contract":"POST /payments/charge { orderId, amount, currency, idempotencyKey } → { paymentId, status, failureReason? }","coupling_type":"synchronous-temporal (current), should migrate to asynchronous-eventual"},{"from":"Payment Service","to":"Stripe","contract":"Stripe Charges API via SDK with idempotency key passthrough","coupling_type":"synchronous-temporal (unavoidable for real-time charge, but must be wrapped with circuit breaker)"},{"from":"Order Service","to":"Notification Service","contract":"POST /notifications/send { orderId, type, recipient, payload } — currently fire-and-forget","coupling_type":"fire-and-forget HTTP (current), should migrate to transactional outbox + message broker"}],"data_flow":"Customer → Order Service (create order, set PAYMENT_PENDING) → Payment Service (charge with idempotency key) → Stripe (process card) → Payment Service returns result → Order Service (set CONFIRMED or FAILED) → Notification Service (send email). Current flow is a synchronous chain for payment and a detached fire-and-forget for notification.","patterns_used":[{"pattern":"Circuit Breaker","rationale":"Prevents cascading failure when Payment Service or Stripe is down. Order Service stops calling Payment Service after N consecutive failures, fails fast, and periodically probes for recovery."},{"pattern":"Idempotency Keys","rationale":"Critical for safe retries across the entire chain. Order Service generates a deterministic idempotency key per order attempt. Payment Service forwards it to Stripe. Prevents double-charging on retries after ambiguous failures (timeouts, network partitions)."},{"pattern":"Transactional Outbox","rationale":"Replaces fire-and-forget notification calls. Order Service writes a notification event to an outbox table in the same transaction as the order state change. A relay process publishes it to a message broker. Guarantees at-least-once delivery."},{"pattern":"Timeout + Retry with Exponential Backoff and Jitter","rationale":"Prevents indefinite blocking on synchronous calls. Jitter prevents thundering herd on recovery. Retries only safe with idempotency keys in place."},{"pattern":"Bulkhead Isolation","rationale":"Isolates the HTTP connection pool used for Payment Service calls from the pool used for other downstream calls, preventing a slow Payment Service from exhausting all connections."},{"pattern":"Saga / Compensating Transaction","rationale":"If post-payment steps fail (e.g., order confirmation write fails after payment succeeds), a compensation step (refund) is triggered. Ensures the system doesn't charge customers for orders it can't fulfill."},{"pattern":"Dead Letter Queue","rationale":"Failed notifications and failed payment events that exhaust retries are routed to a DLQ for manual inspection and replay, preventing silent data loss."},{"pattern":"Health Check / Fallback","rationale":"Payment Service exposes a health endpoint. Order Service can degrade gracefully (queue orders for later processing) when Payment Service is unhealthy, rather than rejecting all orders."}]},"domain_model":{"bounded_contexts":[{"name":"Ordering","classification":"core","aggregates":["Order (root: orderId, states: CREATED → PAYMENT_PENDING → CONFIRMED → FAILED → COMPENSATING → REFUNDED)"]},{"name":"Payment","classification":"core","aggregates":["Payment (root: paymentId, states: INITIATED → CHARGED → FAILED → REFUNDED, invariant: exactly-once charge per idempotency key)"]},{"name":"Notification","classification":"supporting","aggregates":["NotificationRequest (root: notificationId, states: PENDING → SENT → FAILED)"]}],"context_map":[{"upstream":"Payment","downstream":"Ordering","relationship":"Customer-Supplier. Payment Service defines the charge API; Order Service consumes it. Payment Service must not break contract without coordinating with Order Service."},{"upstream":"Stripe (External)","downstream":"Payment","relationship":"Conformist with Anti-Corruption Layer. Payment Service conforms to Stripe's model but wraps it behind an ACL to isolate Stripe-specific concepts (tokens, webhook shapes) from the internal payment model."},{"upstream":"Ordering","downstream":"Notification","relationship":"Customer-Supplier via async events. Notification conforms to order events. Decoupled by outbox + broker."}]},"trade_offs":[{"decision":"Keep Order→Payment synchronous (with resilience patterns) rather than going fully async","gains":"Customer gets immediate payment confirmation in the HTTP response. Simpler UX. No polling or webhook needed on the frontend.","sacrifices":"Availability is bounded by Payment Service uptime. Order Service thread is blocked during payment. Higher coupling.","alternatives_considered":["Fully async payment with polling/webhooks — better availability but significantly more complex UX and requires frontend changes","Reserve-then-capture pattern — two-phase where order is confirmed optimistically and payment captured async — good for some domains but adds complexity and settlement delay"]},{"decision":"Transactional Outbox over direct message broker publish","gains":"Atomicity between order state change and notification event. No dual-write problem. Guaranteed at-least-once delivery.","sacrifices":"Requires an outbox table and a polling/CDC relay process. Adds operational complexity. Slight latency increase for notifications.","alternatives_considered":["Direct publish to broker after DB commit — risks message loss if process crashes between commit and publish","Change Data Capture (CDC) on order table — elegant but requires infrastructure like Debezium and adds operational dependency"]},{"decision":"Circuit breaker at Order→Payment boundary","gains":"Prevents cascade failure. Fails fast when Payment is down. Allows graceful degradation.","sacrifices":"During open-circuit state, orders cannot be processed (unless a fallback queue is implemented). Tuning thresholds requires production data.","alternatives_considered":["No circuit breaker, rely on timeouts only — simpler but risks thread/connection pool exhaustion under sustained Payment failure"]}],"flexibility_points":[{"area":"Payment Gateway","evolution_path":"The ACL in Payment Service isolates Stripe specifics. Switching to Adyen, Braintree, or adding multi-gateway routing requires changes only inside Payment Service. No Order Service changes needed."},{"area":"Notification Channel","evolution_path":"With the outbox/broker pattern, adding SMS, push notifications, or in-app messaging means adding new consumers on the notification topic. Zero changes to Order Service."},{"area":"Payment Flow","evolution_path":"Can evolve from synchronous charge to reserve-then-capture or fully async payment by changing only the Order-Payment interface and adding a webhook handler. The idempotency key infrastructure supports all these patterns."},{"area":"Retry Policy","evolution_path":"Retry counts, backoff multipliers, and circuit breaker thresholds should be externalized to configuration (feature flags or config service) for tuning without redeployment."}],"rigidity_points":[{"area":"Synchronous Order→Payment call path","reason":"As long as this remains synchronous, Order Service availability is capped by Payment Service availability. This is an intentional trade-off for UX simplicity but is the primary rigidity constraint in the system."},{"area":"Idempotency key generation","reason":"The key generation scheme (e.g., orderId + attemptNumber) must be defined early and maintained consistently. Changing it later risks double-charges or missed charges during rollout."},{"area":"Order state machine","reason":"The state transitions (especially CONFIRMED→COMPENSATING→REFUNDED) encode business-critical invariants. Changes here require careful coordination across Order and Payment services."}],"risks":[{"risk":"Ambiguous failure on Payment Service timeout — Order Service doesn't know if Stripe charged the card","severity":"critical","mitigation":"Idempotency keys are mandatory. On timeout: (1) Order stays in PAYMENT_PENDING, (2) retry with same idempotency key, (3) Stripe deduplicates. Payment Service must also implement a status-check endpoint so Order Service can query payment state after timeout."},{"risk":"Silent notification loss with fire-and-forget HTTP POST","severity":"high","mitigation":"Replace immediately with transactional outbox pattern. Until then, add response status logging and a reconciliation job that detects orders without corresponding notification delivery records."},{"risk":"Cascading failure — Stripe outage causes Payment Service to exhaust thread pool, which causes Order Service to exhaust its thread pool, which causes customer-facing API to become unresponsive","severity":"critical","mitigation":"Three-layer defense: (1) Timeouts on every synchronous call (Order→Payment: 5s, Payment→Stripe: 10s), (2) Circuit breaker at Order→Payment boundary, (3) Bulkhead isolation for payment-related connection pool."},{"risk":"Double charging — retry after ambiguous failure without idempotency key","severity":"critical","mitigation":"Generate idempotency key deterministically from orderId before first payment attempt. Store it on the order record. Pass it through entire chain to Stripe. Never retry without it."},{"risk":"Zombie PAYMENT_PENDING orders — payment times out, retry also fails, order stuck in limbo","severity":"high","mitigation":"Background reconciliation job runs every N minutes, queries Payment Service for status of PAYMENT_PENDING orders older than threshold, and transitions them to CONFIRMED or FAILED based on actual payment state."},{"risk":"Circuit breaker tuning — too aggressive opens circuit on transient blips, too conservative doesn't protect against real outages","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Start with conservative defaults (5 failures in 60s window, 30s open duration). Instrument with metrics. Tune based on real production traffic patterns. Consider using a sliding window rather than count-based."},{"risk":"Partial failure in saga — payment succeeds but order DB write fails, customer charged for non-existent order","severity":"high","mitigation":"Implement compensating transaction: if order confirmation write fails after successful payment, trigger refund via Payment Service. Log compensation events to DLQ for audit. Alert on any compensation trigger."}],"implementation_guidance":{"recommended_approach":"Phase the rollout in three stages. Phase 1 (immediate, high-ROI): Add idempotency keys end-to-end and timeouts on all synchronous calls. Phase 2 (short-term): Add circuit breaker at Order→Payment, replace fire-and-forget notifications with transactional outbox, add PAYMENT_PENDING reconciliation job. Phase 3 (medium-term): Add bulkhead isolation, dead letter queues, and comprehensive observability (distributed tracing, circuit breaker state dashboards, payment reconciliation metrics).","files_to_create_or_modify":["order-service/src/payment-client.ts — Add timeout, retry with backoff, circuit breaker wrapper, idempotency key generation","order-service/src/outbox/ — Outbox table migration, outbox writer, outbox relay/poller","order-service/src/order-state-machine.ts — Add COMPENSATING and REFUNDED states, compensation logic","payment-service/src/charge-handler.ts — Accept and enforce idempotency key, add Stripe idempotency passthrough","payment-service/src/stripe-acl.ts — Anti-corruption layer isolating Stripe SDK specifics","payment-service/src/status-endpoint.ts — Payment status query for reconciliation","notification-service/src/consumer.ts — Message broker consumer replacing HTTP endpoint","shared/circuit-breaker.ts — Reusable circuit breaker implementation (or use existing library like opossum)","infra/reconciliation-job.ts — Cron job to resolve zombie PAYMENT_PENDING orders"],"patterns_to_follow":"Use the existing service's error handling conventions. Circuit breaker and retry should be implemented as middleware/wrapper around the HTTP client, not embedded in business logic. Idempotency keys are generated at the domain layer (Order aggregate), not in the HTTP client. The outbox relay should use polling initially (simpler ops) with a migration path to CDC later.","pitfalls_to_avoid":["DO NOT retry payment calls without idempotency keys — this is the #1 cause of double-charging in distributed payment systems","DO NOT catch and swallow timeout errors — an ambiguous timeout must be treated as 'unknown state', not as failure","DO NOT put circuit breaker inside Payment Service — it belongs in the calling service (Order Service) to protect the caller's resources","DO NOT use in-memory circuit breaker state in a multi-instance deployment without considering that each instance has independent state — this is usually acceptable but be aware of it","DO NOT implement retry at multiple layers (HTTP client AND application layer) — pick one layer to own retry logic to avoid retry amplification (e.g., 3 retries × 3 retries = 9 calls)","DO NOT make the outbox relay and the order write use different database transactions — the entire point is atomicity"]},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":92,"high_confidence_areas":["Failure mode enumeration — these are well-known distributed systems failure patterns","Idempotency key requirement — this is non-negotiable for payment systems","Transactional outbox over fire-and-forget — clear improvement with well-understood trade-offs","Circuit breaker placement and rationale"],"low_confidence_areas":["Specific timeout values — these depend on observed P99 latencies in production","Circuit breaker thresholds — require production traffic data to tune properly","Whether synchronous payment is the right long-term choice — depends on UX requirements and business tolerance for async confirmation"],"assumptions":["The system uses a relational database for Order Service (needed for transactional outbox)","Stripe is the only payment gateway currently (single ACL implementation)","Order volumes are moderate enough that a polling-based outbox relay is sufficient (vs. CDC)","The team has operational capacity to manage a message broker (e.g., SQS, RabbitMQ, or Kafka)"]}}

Order Service stops calling Payment Service after N consecutive failures, fails fast, and periodically probes for recovery."},{"pattern":"Idempotency Keys","rationale":"Critical for safe retries across the entire chain. Prevents double-charging on retries after ambiguous failures (timeouts, network partitions)."},{"pattern":"Transactional Outbox","rationale":"Replaces fire-and-forget notification calls. Order Service writes a notification event to an outbox table in the same transaction as the order state change. A relay process publishes it to a message broker. Guarantees at-least-once delivery."},{"pattern":"Timeout + Retry with Exponential Backoff and Jitter","rationale":"Prevents indefinite blocking on synchronous calls. Jitter prevents thundering herd on recovery. Retries only safe with idempotency keys in place."},{"pattern":"Bulkhead Isolation","rationale":"Isolates the HTTP connection pool used for Payment Service calls from the pool used for other downstream calls, preventing a slow Payment Service from exhausting all connections."},{"pattern":"Saga / Compensating Transaction","rationale":"If post-payment steps fail (e.g., order confirmation write fails after payment succeeds), a compensation step (refund) is triggered. Ensures the system doesn't charge customers for orders it can't fulfill."},{"pattern":"Dead Letter Queue","rationale":"Failed notifications and failed payment events that exhaust retries are routed to a DLQ for manual inspection and replay, preventing silent data loss."},{"pattern":"Health Check / Fallback","rationale":"Payment Service exposes a health endpoint. Order Service can degrade gracefully (queue orders for later processing) when Payment Service is unhealthy, rather than rejecting all orders."}]},"domain_model":{"bounded_contexts":[{"name":"Ordering","classification":"core","aggregates":["Order (root: orderId, states: CREATED → PAYMENT_PENDING → CONFIRMED → FAILED → COMPENSATING → REFUNDED)"]},{"name":"Payment","classification":"core","aggregates":["Payment (root: paymentId, states: INITIATED → CHARGED → FAILED → REFUNDED, invariant: exactly-once charge per idempotency key)"]},{"name":"Notification","classification":"supporting","aggregates":["NotificationRequest (root: notificationId, states: PENDING → SENT → FAILED)"]}],"context_map":[{"upstream":"Payment","downstream":"Ordering","relationship":"Customer-Supplier. Payment Service must not break contract without coordinating with Order Service."},{"upstream":"Stripe (External)","downstream":"Payment","relationship":"Conformist with Anti-Corruption Layer. Notification conforms to order events. Zero changes to Order Service."},{"area":"Payment Flow","evolution_path":"Can evolve from synchronous charge to reserve-then-capture or fully async payment by changing only the Order-Payment interface and adding a webhook handler.
